paper_title: MERA: Model Evolution and Routing with Skill Adaptation for Agentic Systems at Scale
status: provisional — A800 offline day 71; GRPO results n=1 seed; v7 (2026-07-24): +Algorithm 1 MERA Cycle box (closes W7); +per-cycle skills arm and ACR in Table 9 (new data); +Hypothesis D (GRPO forgetting, arxiv:2607.04364) in §5.4; +rise-and-collapse (arxiv:2606.21090) in §2; +EXP-132/133 queued; §9 Reproducibility paths fixed; AAAI deadline 22 days — RUSH MODE ACTIVE. July 20 minviable restore date PASSED.

new_findings_2026_07_24.algorithm1_box: Algorithm 1 MERA Cycle box added to §3.4 — closes W7 from review history. Formal pseudocode covering: signature extraction, router routing on raw prompt, procedure-augmented generation, SkillBook update, Phase 3a SFT, Phase 3b GRPO with DAPO dynamic sampling, Phase 4 router update. 4 design invariants annotated.
new_findings_2026_07_24.per_cycle_skills_and_acr: Table 9 expanded: per-cycle skills arm added for ALL cycles (was only cycle 0 and 3). Cycle 1 skills arm = 65.85% (dip from 70.73% in cycle 0) — non-monotonic behavior not previously reported. Per-cycle ACR from grpo_info.json: 51.2% (c0), 47.6% (c1), 46.3% (c2), 52.4% (c3). Data shows ACR and skills arm improvement are anti-correlated in cycle 1, weakening pure ACR explanation.
new_findings_2026_07_24.hypothesis_d_grpo_forgetting: Hypothesis D added to §5.4: arxiv:2607.04364 (CPO 'RL Forgets!') grounds GRPO forgetting of SFT gains as complementary Full=Router explanation. Easy tasks with all-pass rollouts (advantage=0) receive no GRPO gradient but can have their SFT-trained weights degraded by hard-task GRPO updates. L2-SP regularization (EXP-130) tests this directly.
new_findings_2026_07_24.hypothesis_e_rise_collapse: Hypothesis E added to §5.4: arxiv:2606.21090 documents pass@1 peaking at step 10-20 and collapsing within the same GRPO run. If cycle adapters are final-step collapsed checkpoints, Full=Router regardless of algorithm. EXP-132 tests this with per-step eval logging — zero algorithm change fix if confirmed.
new_findings_2026_07_24.rise_collapse_related_work: §2 Continual Learning subsection expanded: +CPO (arxiv:2607.04364) formal forgetting proof, +rise-and-collapse (arxiv:2606.21090) failure mode. Both ground two new hypotheses D/E that deepen the Full=Router analysis.
new_findings_2026_07_24.reproducibility_paths_fixed: §9 Reproducibility: outdated paths to removed experiments/ tree corrected to current src/pipeline/ layout. Added grpo_info.json citation and smoke test command.
new_findings_2026_07_24.exp_132_queued: EXP-132: Rise-and-Collapse Diagnostic (priority=8, gpu=~3h) — per-step GRPO eval logging + best-epoch checkpoint selection. Tests Hypothesis E.
new_findings_2026_07_24.exp_133_queued: EXP-133: Per-Cycle ACR-vs-Improvement Correlation Analysis (priority=7, gpu=0h) — offline analysis using existing grpo_info.json data. Tests whether ACR predicts skills arm improvement; preliminary data suggests anti-correlation in cycle 1.

strongest_claims.he_4cycle_skills_arm: Skills arm improves 70.73%->75.61% over 4 DAPO cycles on HumanEval
strongest_claims.he_4cycle_routing: Full system 92.68% routing accuracy at 27.56% cost (cycle 3)
strongest_claims.tau2_joint_peak: 89.19% task pass at 22.16% of always-large cost (Cycle 1, joint run)
strongest_claims.tau2_small_dominates: 35B MoE 80% > GPT-5.4 71% on held-out tau2 tasks
strongest_claims.zero_variance_theory: 52.4% zero-variance DAPO groups = provably zero gradient (arxiv:2507.05386)

new_findings_2026_07_03.rft_forgetting_theory: arxiv:2507.05386 formally proves: RFT gradient ∝ reward variance → zero-variance groups are training no-ops. Theoretically grounds our 52.4% zero-variance finding.
new_findings_2026_07_03.gmpo_remedy: arxiv:2507.20673 GMPO geometric mean bounding outlier gradient at syntax-critical tokens — orthogonal remedy for 47.6% non-collapsed groups.
new_findings_2026_07_03.tau2_sft_forgetting: New connection: tau2 cycle oscillation (89.19%→70.27%) is consistent with SFT catastrophic forgetting (arxiv:2507.05386) — SFT not RFT used for tau2 adapter.
new_findings_2026_07_03.multitrack_framing: Explicit §1 framing: Tracks A-D are ablation studies at different modalities/scales, not simultaneous deployments.

new_findings_2026_07_17.group_sd_identity: arxiv:2607.00152 (Bay & Yearick) proves GRPO/Dr.GRPO/DAPO are three operations on σ. Dr.GRPO drops the 1/σ division → update ∝ p*(1-p), naturally weighting informative prompts. Our 52.4% zero-variance finding corresponds exactly to the σ=0 regime. Added to §2 Related Work, §7 Zero-Variance analysis, Abstract. New bib key: groupsd2026.
new_findings_2026_07_17.zpd_sft: arxiv:2507.00054 reward-guided distillation proposes ZPD filtering (r_small ∈ [0.2, 0.8]) to target teacher traces where student has partial capability. Directly addresses our 20.7pp skills gap. Added to §2 Related Work, §7 Skills Gap paragraph, §7 Remedies. New bib key: zpddistill2026.
new_findings_2026_07_17.exp_122_queued: EXP-122: Joint ZPD-SFT + Dr.GRPO hybrid test — addresses A+C simultaneously; first experiment to test interaction of two orthogonal fixes. Expected: if skills arm ≥78%, joint fix narrative is paper's strongest new result.
new_findings_2026_07_17.exp_123_queued: EXP-123: Group-SD ceiling analysis — use per-cycle σ distribution to compute theoretical max gradient contribution under Dr.GRPO vs GRPO; zero-GPU data analysis on existing trace logs.

EXP-100: tau2 3-seed (n=3) for CI on all tau2 tables — blocked on A800 (AAAI CRITICAL)
EXP-132: Rise-and-collapse diagnostic — save per-step GRPO checkpoints and eval; tests Hypothesis E; ~3h GPU — blocked on A800 (AAAI HIGH)
EXP-066: tau2 router regularization to fix held-out overfitting — blocked on A800
EXP-080: multi-seed 4-cycle HumanEval validation — blocked on A800
EXP-077: DAPO G=16 DDT to reduce 52.4% zero-variance — blocked on A800
EXP-106: tau2 random-50% routing baseline — blocked on A800 (W5)
EXP-107: cross-domain router eval — blocked on A800 (W4)

Paper is submittable NOW with n=1 seed + SE caveats. 22 days to deadline: submit-ready draft August 8, buffer to Aug 15.
aaai_timeline_risk.recommended_action: Final GPU experiment window closes August 1. If A800 restores before August 1: EXP-132 first (2h, rise-and-collapse diagnostic, no algorithm change), then EXP-099 (3-seed HumanEval, 4h). If A800 down through August 1: submit with n=1 seed + explicit SE bars (±5.4pp at 82 tasks, ±5pp at 100 tasks). Begin final submission polish now — target complete draft August 8.
